opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My alarm clock went off really loudly this morning, and the next thing I remember is waking up on the floor with my roommate shaking me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden collapse immediately after being startled by a loud alarm, witnessed by the roommate, with brief stiffening and rapid recovery.

symptom_narrative.onset: suddenly, immediately after a loud alarm sounded
symptom_narrative.duration: brief - about 10 seconds of stiffening then full recovery within a minute
symptom_narrative.character: abrupt loss of consciousness with no warning, brief stiffening, then waking on the floor
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: frightening - a complete blackout with no warning
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: collapsed to the floor; no localised symptom
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: triggered immediately by the loud acoustic stimulus (fire-alarm testing in the building)
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the loud noise/startle triggered it; the alarm testing is still ongoing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest pain or palpitations beforehand; brief stiffening witnessed; no tongue-biting or prolonged confusion afterwards
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none that she recalls; this is the first collapse
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: fully recovered and independent now, but very shaken

family_history_as_reported.narrative: a maternal aunt died suddenly of 'unknown causes' at age 25

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with a roommate; the building's fire alarm is still undergoing intermittent testing

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened by the sudden unprovoked collapse and worried about it happening again

communication_style.typical_message_style: clear account with the roommate corroborating the witnessed event
